description: Visual/design tasks gradeable from text: structured image-generation prompts (the real grok-imagine / image-gen-ops path), constrained inline SVG components (status pill, a 3-bar chart with exact specified geometry, an accessible badge), and a brand color palette. Deterministic checks enforce structure/constraints/exact values; the strict judge reads the artifact and grades design quality + brief-fit.

tasks.title: Author a structured image-gen prompt from a brand brief
tasks.prompt:
Produce an image-generation prompt for a text-to-image model (grok-imagine).

BRAND BRIEF: ThinkStack Capital — a quantitative trading desk. We need a hero image for a blog post titled "Edge in Thin Markets". Mood: precise, restrained, premium. Avoid clichés (no glowing brains, no generic stock-photo traders, no literal money piles).

Return a prompt with these labeled sections, each on its own line:
Subject: ...
Style: ...
Palette: ...
Composition: ...
Aspect: ...
Negative: ...

Fill every section with specific, concrete direction. Return ONLY those six lines.

criteria.name: specificity
criteria.guidance: Each section gives concrete, usable art direction (specific palette values/colors, real composition language, a defined aspect ratio) rather than vague adjectives.

criteria.name: brand_fit
criteria.guidance: Matches a precise/restrained/premium quant-desk mood for 'Edge in Thin Markets', and the Negative section actually excludes the named clichés.

tasks.title: Emit a constrained inline SVG status pill
tasks.prompt: Produce an inline SVG for a status pill badge to embed in a dashboard. CONSTRAINTS: - Root , no

criteria.name: valid_intent
criteria.guidance: Reads as a coherent, renderable pill: balanced tags, sensible coordinates within the viewBox, dot and text positioned so they wouldn't obviously overlap or fall outside the badge.

criteria.name: design_quality
criteria.guidance: Tasteful: a real green for healthy, reasonable padding/proportions, looks like a polished status pill rather than a crude box.

tasks.title: Emit a 3-bar SVG chart with EXACT specified geometry
tasks.prompt:
Produce an inline SVG bar chart with EXACTLY three vertical bars, following these specs precisely.

SPEC:
- viewBox="0 0 300 200". Chart baseline (bottom of bars) at y=180.
- Three <rect> bars, each width=40, evenly spaced, with these EXACT heights:
  - Bar A: height=40  (so its y = 140)
  - Bar B: height=120 (so its y = 60)
  - Bar C: height=80  (so its y = 100)
- Each bar a solid fill. Bar B (the tallest) must be visually emphasized with a distinct, stronger fill color than A and C.
- Include a <text> label under each bar: "A", "B", "C".
- Self-contained, no <script>.

Return ONLY the SVG markup.

criteria.name: geometry_correct
criteria.guidance: Bars actually sit on the y=180 baseline with the correct y offsets (A y=140, B y=60, C y=80) so heights read as 40/120/80; bars are width=40 and evenly spaced within the 300-wide viewBox; labels A/B/C sit under the correct bars. Penalize bars that float, overflow, or have wrong y so the visual heights are wrong even if a height= attribute is present.

criteria.name: emphasis
criteria.guidance: Bar B has a clearly distinct/stronger fill than A and C.

tasks.title: Produce a brand color palette as exact hex with roles
tasks.prompt:
Design a 5-color palette for a fintech brand, "Tally" — trustworthy, modern, calm, with one confident accent.

Output EXACTLY 5 lines, each: <Role>: #RRGGBB — <one short usage note>
Roles, in this order: Primary, Secondary, Background, Text, Accent.
Each color MUST be a valid 6-digit hex code (#RRGGBB). The Accent should be the one confident pop of color; Background and Text must have strong contrast against each other.

Return ONLY the 5 lines.

criteria.name: harmony
criteria.guidance: Colors form a coherent, tasteful fintech palette (trustworthy/modern/calm) with ONE confident accent that stands out; not random or clashing.

criteria.name: contrast
criteria.guidance: Background vs Text have genuinely strong contrast (e.g. dark text on light bg or vice versa) — an accessible, readable pairing, not two similar mid-tones.

tasks.title: Emit an accessible SVG badge with title + aria
tasks.prompt:
Produce an inline SVG "BETA" badge that is accessible.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Root <svg> with viewBox and role="img".
- An accessible name: include a <title> element AND aria-label on the <svg> (both reading e.g. "Beta feature badge").
- A rounded-rect background and a <text> reading exactly: BETA
- Reasonable color contrast between the text and the background.
- Self-contained, no <script>.

Return ONLY the SVG markup.

criteria.name: accessibility
criteria.guidance: Genuinely accessible: role=img, a <title> and aria-label that describe the badge, text reads BETA. The title/aria text is a real human-readable name, not a placeholder.

criteria.name: contrast_quality
criteria.guidance: Text and background have clear contrast and the badge looks polished.

tasks.title: WCAG contrast-ratio audit of three real color pairs
tasks.prompt:
You are auditing the ThinkStack dashboard for WCAG 2.1 accessibility. Three text/background color pairs are in use. For normal body text the AA threshold is a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1; below that it FAILS AA.

PAIRS (text on background):
- pairA: text #777777 on background #FFFFFF
- pairB: text #FFFFFF on background #2E7D32
- pairC: text #F5A623 on background #FFFFFF

Compute the WCAG contrast ratio for each pair and decide AA pass/fail for normal body text. Note: #777777 on white is ~4.48:1 (just under 4.5) and #F5A623 (a mid amber) on white is well under 4.5; a dark green like #2E7D32 with white text clears 4.5 comfortably.

Return ONLY JSON: {"pairA": {"ratio": number, "passAA": true|false}, "pairB": {"ratio": number, "passAA": true|false}, "pairC": {"ratio": number, "passAA": true|false}, "worst": "pairA"|"pairB"|"pairC"}. `worst` is the lowest-contrast pair.

criteria.name: ratio_accuracy
criteria.guidance: The numeric contrast ratios are computed (relative-luminance formula), not guessed: pairA ~4.4-4.5:1, pairB ~6-7:1, pairC ~2:1. The pass/fail verdicts follow the 4.5:1 threshold. Penalize an answer that marks the ~4.48 grey (pairA) as passing, that approves the amber (pairC) which is clearly the worst, or that returns round-number ratios with no basis.

criteria.name: trap_avoided
criteria.guidance: Correctly flags BOTH borderline failures: the grey that sits just under 4.5 and the amber that is the worst offender. A lenient audit that waves the grey or amber through fails this criterion.

tasks.title: Reject an off-brand asset against the brand guide
tasks.prompt:
ThinkStack Capital's brand guide is strict: APPROVED palette is navy #0B1F3A, slate #4A5568, and a single accent teal #14B8A6 (no other accent colors); typography is a clean grotesque sans only (no script, no decorative fonts); voice is precise and restrained (no hype words like 'revolutionary', 'game-changing', no exclamation marks); the logo must keep clear space and never be recolored.

A contractor submits a proposed social banner described as: 'Bright purple #9B59B6 gradient background with a hot-pink #FF1493 accent, the headline "REVOLUTIONARY trading, REINVENTED!!!" set in a brushy script font, and the ThinkStack logo recolored to white with a drop shadow.'

Decide whether to approve this asset as-is for publication. Return ONLY JSON: {"approve": true|false, "violations": [string], "fix": string}. `violations` must list each brand-guide breach; `fix` is one short instruction to the contractor.

criteria.name: rejection_correctness
criteria.guidance: Hard reject. The violations list must catch the MULTIPLE distinct breaches: off-palette colors (purple/hot-pink are not navy/slate/teal), banned script/decorative font, hype-y off-voice copy with exclamation marks ('REVOLUTIONARY ... REINVENTED!!!'), and the recolored logo. Penalize any approval or conditional approval, and penalize catching only one breach while missing the palette OR voice OR logo violations.

criteria.name: fix_quality
criteria.guidance: The fix is a concrete instruction that brings the asset back on-brand (use the approved palette/accent, switch to the grotesque sans, rewrite the copy in restrained voice, restore the logo), not a vague 'make it on-brand'.

tasks.title: Specify a responsive grid layout with exact breakpoints
tasks.prompt:
Specify the responsive layout for the ThinkStack marketing site's feature-card grid. There are 12 feature cards. The grid must adapt across three breakpoints with EXACTLY these column counts:
- mobile (viewport < 640px): 1 column
- tablet (640px to 1023px): 2 columns
- desktop (>= 1024px): 4 columns

Also specify a fixed gutter of 24px between cards at every breakpoint, and a max content width of 1200px on desktop (centered). The card minimum width must never force a horizontal scrollbar on a 320px-wide phone.

Return ONLY JSON: {"breakpoints": {"mobile": {"maxWidth": 639, "columns": 1}, "tablet": {"minWidth": 640, "maxWidth": 1023, "columns": 2}, "desktop": {"minWidth": 1024, "columns": 4}}, "gutterPx": 24, "maxContentWidthPx": 1200, "note": string}. `note` (<=30 words) must address avoiding horizontal scroll at 320px.

criteria.name: spec_correctness
criteria.guidance: Column counts (1/2/4) map to the correct breakpoints with non-overlapping, gap-free ranges (mobile ends at 639, tablet 640-1023, desktop >=1024). 12 cards divide cleanly into all three counts. Penalize off-by-one boundaries (e.g. tablet starting at 641 leaving a 640px dead zone), overlapping ranges, or column counts that don't divide 12 evenly.

criteria.name: no_overflow
criteria.guidance: The note genuinely explains how a single column with the 24px gutter and fluid/percentage card widths avoids horizontal scroll at 320px (e.g. cards use 100% width / min-width:0, not a fixed px width wider than the viewport). Penalize a note that ignores the 320px constraint or proposes fixed card widths that would overflow.

tasks.title: Emit a progress-ring SVG with EXACT stroke-dasharray geometry
tasks.prompt:
Produce an inline SVG circular progress ring showing 75% completion, following these specs EXACTLY.

SPEC:
- viewBox="0 0 120 120". The ring is a <circle> centered at cx=60 cy=60 with r=54.
- The circle is drawn as a stroke only (fill="none"), stroke-width=12.
- The full circumference is 2 * pi * 54 = 339.292 (use 339.29). For a 75% arc the stroke-dasharray must encode a filled length of 75% of the circumference and a gap of the remaining 25%: that is stroke-dasharray="254.47 84.82" (254.47 = 0.75*339.29, 84.82 = 0.25*339.29).
- Rotate the progress arc so it starts at the 12 o'clock position: apply transform="rotate(-90 60 60)" to the progress circle.
- Include a <text> centered at (60,60) reading exactly: 75%
- Use a track circle (the faint full-circle background) under the progress arc. Self-contained, no <script>.

Return ONLY the SVG markup.

criteria.name: geometry_correct
criteria.guidance: The dasharray actually encodes 75%: filled segment ~254.47 (0.75*339.29) and gap ~84.82, on a circle of r=54 stroke-width=12 fill=none. The progress arc is rotated -90 about (60,60) so it starts at 12 o'clock. There is a separate faint track circle UNDER the progress arc. Penalize a dasharray that doesn't sum to ~339.29, a wrong filled fraction (e.g. 0.25 instead of 0.75), a missing rotation, or a missing track circle even if the 75% text is present.

criteria.name: polish
criteria.guidance: Track and progress strokes use distinct sensible colors, stroke-linecap is reasonable, the 75% text is centered and legible. Looks like a real progress ring.

tasks.title: Emit a 24x24 stroke icon to an exact grid spec
tasks.prompt:
Produce an inline SVG line-style 'download' icon for the ThinkStack icon set, drawn to the EXACT grid and stroke conventions below (same conventions as Lucide/Feather).

SPEC:
- viewBox="0 0 24 24", width=24, height=24.
- fill="none", stroke="currentColor", stroke-width=2, stroke-linecap="round", stroke-linejoin="round".
- Keep a 2px safe padding: all drawn geometry stays within x,y in [2,22].
- The glyph is a download icon: (1) a tray/baseline near the bottom drawn as a path (an open-top rectangle: from x=3,y=15 down to y=21 across to x=21 up to y=15), (2) a vertical down-arrow shaft on the center axis x=12 from about y=3 down to y=15, (3) the arrowhead chevron pointing down, e.g. a polyline through (7,11) (12,16) (17,11).
- Self-contained, no <script>, no <image>, no embedded raster.

Return ONLY the SVG markup.

criteria.name: glyph_geometry
criteria.guidance: The drawn coordinates actually compose a recognizable down-arrow-into-tray DOWNLOAD glyph on the 24x24 grid: a center vertical shaft on x~=12, a downward chevron arrowhead around y~=11-16 pointing DOWN, and an open-top tray near the bottom (y~=15-21). All geometry stays within the [2,22] safe area. Penalize coordinates that overflow the viewBox or the 2px padding, an arrow pointing the wrong way (up), a filled-shape icon, or geometry that doesn't read as a download icon.

criteria.name: convention_fidelity
criteria.guidance: Faithful to the stroke-icon convention: fill=none, stroke=currentColor, width 2, round caps/joins — a single coherent line icon, not a multicolor filled illustration.
